summary: Migrate latex/ to main.tex + main_backup.tex layout; purge old-filename references
body_markdown:
- **Task:** Restructure `latex/` to the new working layout — `main.tex` (working file) + `main_backup.tex` (backup copy) and their two PDFs — and purge every reference to the retired sources.
- **Outcome:** Created `main_backup.tex` (copy of `main.tex`); removed the now-dangling `% !TEX root` magic comment from `main.tex`; deleted `recommenders_v2.{tex,pdf}` and `recommenders_commented.{tex,pdf}` plus stray root aux. Rewrote `.latexmkrc` so `$aux_dir=$out_dir=build` and a `$success_cmd = 'cp %D .'` copies only the finished PDF back to root. Both files recompile clean (6pp each); `latex/` root now holds exactly `.latexmkrc`, `main.tex`, `main_backup.tex`, `main.pdf`, `main_backup.pdf`, `build/`. Updated old-filename references in CLAUDE.md, README.md, notes.md, LATEX_TYPESETTING_EXPLANATION.md, and the Phase 1 notebook intro link.
- **Key decisions:** Per user, DELETE the four old files (git-recoverable) rather than archive. Keep the PDF in root while every other artifact (aux, log, synctex, intermediate PDF) lives in `build/`, achieved via latexmk `$success_cmd`. Scope held to LaTeX + its references after a mid-session correction ('only latex, don't touch scripts'); user then re-authorized finishing the cross-file cleanup including the notebook link.
- **KB changes:** None (no KB import; repo-local docs updated in place).
- **Follow-up:** `.claude/settings.json` and `.claude/settings.local.json` still carry obsolete permission allow-entries naming the old files — harmless dead rules, left untouched (harness config, out of latex scope). Prune if desired.

summary: Bootstrap repo workflow scaffolding + paper-side cleanup + calibration-methodology fix in 1b and 1c
body_markdown:
- **Task:** First substantive session in this repo. Bootstrap the PROJECT_SETUP_WORKFLOW scaffolding (CLAUDE.md, TODO_WORKFLOW.md, HOUSEKEEPING.md, worklog.jsonl, housekeeping_log.jsonl, .gitignore), correct README/LaTeX drift, then fix the posterior-gap calibration in experiments/1b and 1c.
- **Outcome:** Repo is now self-contained (no dependency on sibling latex_repo — macros inlined per .tex source, TEXINPUTS hack removed from .latexmkrc); pytest baseline still 18/18 green; orphan PDFs deleted (root recommenders.pdf + latex/recommenders.pdf had no .tex source); latex/build/ + __pycache__ untracked via git rm --cached and added to a new .gitignore. Notebooks 1b and 1c now use delta = Delta/2 (delta < Delta) so the posterior-gap criterion is asymptotically achievable; 1c N_STEPS bumped 200 -> 1000 for closer-to-convergence calibration; 1c S_GRID extended to {1, 10, 50, 200, 500, 1000, 3000, 10000} because the new threshold pushes the inverted s into the 1000-3000 range.
- **Key decisions:** (1) Vendor LaTeX macros inline in each .tex preamble rather than via a shared macros.sty (user iterated through: latex_repo/shared -> local latex/macros.sty -> fully inline). (2) For posterior-gap calibration, fix delta = Delta/2 as a convention — the old delta = 0.05 fixed-absolute setting put low-Delta cells in the pathology delta > true gap where q -> 0 by finite-T noise rather than tracking genuine discovery. T-convergence diagnostic (s in {10, 50, 150}, T in {200, 1000}, N_AGENTS=250) confirmed q drifts toward 0 in T under the buggy regime. (3) Defer formatter/linter/typechecker adoption — HOUSEKEEPING records them as n/a until configured.
- **KB changes:** None to the central KB — single capture proposed (numpy-2 ABI vs anaconda C-ext deps: numexpr, bottleneck, pyarrow) was declined as too small. This worklog entry is the only kb_mcp write.
- **Follow-up:** User will rerun 1b (~2.5 min) and 1c (~9 min) with the new GAP_DELTA convention. Expected: 1b still reports q=0.05 OUT OF RANGE (symmetry floor near 1/2 is robust to the delta scaling — now an honest finding rather than a criterion-mismatch artefact); 1c reports a finite s in the high-s range. User upgraded anaconda numexpr and bottleneck to NumPy-2-compatible wheels (2.10+ / 1.4+) to silence the loud ABI traceback at imports.
